title: After-Action Report — fast-sim stat repair (wERA archive) + outs-based DIPS leaderboards
scope: The session opened with screenshots of the archived-season leaderboards (wRC+ / WPA / wERA / wERA+ · Top 10) where every value was degenerate: wRC+ identical -23 for all ten batters; WPA +0.00 for every batter and pitcher; wERA 0.00 for everyone; wERA+ 100 for everyone.

rationale.item: The user couldn't tell whether the stats were "always broken or a specific issue with the fast sim context," noted that an ERA+ of 100 (and WPA) told them nothing, and asked for more useful, front-office-grade analytics — specifically flagging that total outs as an IP proxy would be an easy base for new rate stats.
WPA = +0.00 — fast-sim-specific: simulate_game(detail="lite") skips the per-PA event log game_pa_log; build_player_wpa() walks that log.

wRC+ = −23 (constant) — fast-sim-specific: wOBA weights are empirically derived from game_pa_log; empty log → every weight 0 → every batter's wOBA = 0 → wRC+ collapses to one league-determined constant.
wERA = 0.00 / wERA+ = 100 — an archive bug, not fast-sim: season_archive._snapshot_leaders omitted the arc columns from its pitching SELECT, so _aggregate_pitcher_rows read every arc as 0.

Design read: WPA and the pa_log-derived '+' metrics are the least salvageable — reviving them in fast sim means re-simulating every PA, which defeats the point of fast sim. The durable answer is box-score analytics keyed off total outs (IP = outs/3); the gaps were FIP and K/BB, plus the archived boards surfacing the broken stats.

delegation_events.decision: Do not revive pa_log metrics in fast sim by re-simulating every PA
delegation_events.quote: the user explicitly declined that option

delegation_events.decision: Scope of the shipped work (fix wERA archive bug; add FIP + K/BB; swap broken archive columns; substitute OPS+ for wRC+ on the archive)
delegation_events.quote: Scope was confirmed with the user before implementing (fix the wERA archive bug; add FIP + K/BB; swap the broken archive columns; and — in a follow-up turn — substitute OPS+ for wRC+ on the archive).

delegation_events.decision: Box-score-robust substitutes over a minimal lite-mode game_pa_log write
delegation_events.quote: Not done; the user preferred the box-score-robust substitutes.

actions.description: Fixed the archived wERA bug: _snapshot_leaders pitching aggregation now selects arc-bucketed columns (er_arc*/k_arc*/fo_arc*/bf_arc*) plus true-outcome/hit-type columns that _aggregate_pitcher_rows consumes
Added FIP and K/BB to the pitcher aggregate, with new _league_fip_constant() helper anchoring league FIP to league ERA; K/BB left None when no walks

Swapped dead boards on the archived season page: WPA pitching board → FIP/K/BB/WHIP/K9 boards; OPS+ substituted for wRC+ on batting; dropped both WPA boards; new persisted columns fip_dips/kbb/whip_v/k9 and ops_plus added via idempotent ALTER migration pattern + CREATE TABLE defaults; legacy archives fall back to original wRC+ table
Surfaced FIP + K/BB on the live /leaders page — FIP alongside the wERA/xRA run-estimator trio, K/BB with the command rates

risk_posture: Box-score-robust stats over reviving pa_log metrics. ... Chosen over making lite sim write a minimal `game_pa_log` (heavier, slows the very mode that exists to be fast).
OPS+ substitutes for wRC+ on the archive only. wRC+ stays on the live page (correct there).
New, clearly-named archive columns instead of reusing the legacy ones.
`_league_fip_constant()` as its own helper. One extra league query per `_aggregate_pitcher_rows` call ... in exchange for not changing that function's return-tuple arity.

validation_claims.claim: Engine repro: ran 3 games through the core engine — er_arc populated ([25,24,8]), proving the arc data exists independent of lite mode.
validation_claims.check_method: Run 3 engine games and inspect er_arc

validation_claims.claim: Archive repro: same pitcher row aggregates to werra=4.000 with the arc columns vs werra=0.000 without.
validation_claims.check_method: Aggregate one pitcher row with/without arc columns

validation_claims.claim: End-to-end snapshot: wERA (2.01/5.99), wERA+ (199/67), FIP (0.83/7.17), K/BB (11.0/0.60), WHIP, K/9 all populated and correctly ranked; OPS+ board 157 vs 28 where wRC+ was constant.
validation_claims.check_method: Build throwaway DB, run _snapshot_leaders, read back season_pitching_leaders

validation_claims.claim: /leaders and /seasons/1 both return 200; FIP and K/BB cards on /leaders, FIP/K/BB/WHIP/K9 + OPS+ tables on the archive page, WPA / wRC+ tables gone.
tests/test_stat_invariants.py errors with 'no such table: games' — the same pre-existing seeded-DB environment issue noted in the predecessor AAR, unrelated to these changes.
validation_claims.check_method: Run pytest tests/test_stat_invariants.py in a seeded environment

non_actions.item: Reviving wRC+ / WPA in fast sim (e.g. a minimal game_pa_log write under lite)
non_actions.reason: a deliberate, measured slowdown of fast sim. Not done; the user preferred the box-score-robust substitutes.
the card() macro only links to the glossary when has_glossary(key) is true, so the new cards render fine without links

residual_risks: wRC+ / WPA in fast sim remain dead by design.
FIP and xRA now sit side by side on both pages; may read as redundant to players.
Other archive-adjacent aggregations (HoF, team pages) not swept for the 'snapshot SELECT drifted from the aggregator' bug class.
open_hooks: Short glossary.py pass for fip/kbb parity
Consider folding FIP into the XO-crossover block and leaving xRA as the native headline
Sweep to confirm every archive-adjacent aggregation selects the full arc set
section_headers_original: After-Action Report — fast-sim stat repair (wERA archive) + outs-based DIPS leaderboards
unmapped_content:
**Branch:** `claude/sim-ahead-stats-analytics-8eqD0`
**Predecessor:** `aar-ui-retheme-nav-and-web-modularization.md`

Diagnosis note: "Confirmed by reading the code and reproducing each failure against a throwaway DB. The headline finding: a normally-simmed current season viewed live computes all four correctly. The screenshots are the fast-sim + archived-season combination." Reproduced: two hitters with OPS 2.800 vs 0.500 both got wOBA 0.000 and identical wRC+. Verification preamble: "No browser and no seeded DB in this environment, so verification was via throwaway DBs (O27V2_DB_PATH + init_db), the Flask test client, and the stat suite (flask/pytest installed ad hoc)." FIP formula: (13·HR + 3·(BB+HBP) − 2·K)/IP + C; a 27-out frame = 9 IP. The card() macro already filters None via selectattr(key,'ne',None), so walk-less pitchers are simply omitted from the K/BB board (no crash). Career/player-page lines already select the arc columns, so career wERA was unaffected.
parser_notes: Clear AAR with explicit Date completed line.
